title: Aluminum Prices Are Giving Up Some Gains Despite Gulf Disruptions - WSJ
summary: This article says aluminum prices pulled back about 6% to $3,214.50 in early U.S. trading on Thursday after earlier gains. It links the move to softer demand, rising inventories in China, and pressure on Chinese buyers from higher costs, even as Middle East disruptions continue to affect supply chains.

title: Aluminum Prices Hit 6-Month High as Supply Concerns Grow
summary: The article reports aluminum reaching a 6-month high as supply concerns intensified, especially around Chinese output nearing its production cap. It also notes the Midwest Premium hit an all-time high, reinforcing the view that tight supply is supporting prices.

title: Aluminium Price Today - Investing.com
summary: This market page shows aluminum futures trading around the 3,399 to 3,406 range, indicating prices remain elevated. It is a live price feed rather than a traditional news article, but it is useful for tracking the latest market level and short-term movement.

title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News - Trading Economics
summary: Trading Economics reports aluminum rose to 3,518.48 USD/T on June 11, 2026, up 1.24% from the previous day. It also notes that while the price declined over the past month, it remained substantially higher year over year.

title: Aluminium Prices Are On The Rise As US Premium At Record High
summary: The segment says aluminum prices are being pushed higher by supply concerns, Chinese production limits, and a market deficit. It also mentions strong demand in Europe and rising U.S. premiums, both of which support a bullish market trend.

title: Major US aluminum producer boosts production to full capacity
summary: This report says a major U.S. aluminum producer is increasing output to full capacity amid market and policy changes. It is presented as a recent update on production levels rather than a broad industry analysis.

title: Century Aluminum CEO details impact of trade moves
summary: The video segment focuses on Century Aluminum’s response to trade moves and its manufacturing capacity. It mentions a return to 100% capacity and references new jobs tied to expanded production.

title: America's big new aluminum smelter is still waiting on a power deal
summary: This article discusses Century Aluminum’s planned new smelter, which is expected to produce up to 750,000 metric tons annually. The piece centers on the project’s progress and the need for a power agreement before it can move forward.

title: New filter captures and recycles aluminum from manufacturing waste
summary: MIT engineers developed a nanofiltration process that can capture more than 99% of aluminum ions from waste streams similar to those produced by aluminum plants. The approach could increase aluminum yield while reducing hazardous waste and improving sustainability in production.

title: Steel prices, news & market analysis
summary: Argus reports that steel market participants expect a slower, lower-margin second half of 2026 after a supply-led rally in prices during the first half of the year. The page also provides steel commodity price coverage and market forecasts across related ferrous inputs and products.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Trading Economics reports that steel prices fell to 3,084 CNY/T on June 18, 2026, down 0.06% from the previous day. It also notes that prices are down 3.08% over the past month but remain 4.37% higher over the past year, indicating mixed short-term and longer-term momentum.

title: Steel Market Update: Home
summary: Steel Market Update says the average domestic hot rolled coil price was $1,130 per short ton this week, up $15 per short ton week over week. The update suggests prices remained firm in both domestic and offshore markets, reflecting continued market tightness.

title: Steel Price News: Are Prices About to Drop? Latest Hot Rolled Coil
summary: Ryerson’s market intelligence page lists current U.S. steel prices, including hot-rolled coil at $1,109 per ton and other product categories such as cold rolled and coated steel. The page frames the coverage around whether prices are likely to drop, signaling attention to near-term market direction.

title: Steel Market Prices, Data, and Analysis
summary: S&P Global provides steel market prices, historical and spot data, and real-time market coverage. The page is a market analysis resource rather than a single news story, but it is relevant for current pricing and trend tracking.

title: Steel production up 44.4% year over year after pandemic plunge
source: YouTube / reporting based on American Iron and Steel Institute data
summary: This report says Great Lakes steel production rose by 5,000 tons last week, while U.S. steel mills remained close to 80% of capacity. It frames the increase as part of a rebound from the pandemic-era slump in steel output.

title: In the week ending on June 13, 2026, domestic raw steel production was 1,854,000 net tons while the capability utilization rate was 80.3 percent
summary: AISI’s latest weekly industry data reports U.S. domestic raw steel production at 1,854,000 net tons, with capability utilization at 80.3 percent. This provides the most direct recent snapshot of U.S. steel production and capacity usage.

title: Global steel industry battles surplus supply, protectionism
summary: The article says global steelmaking capacity is expected to keep rising, reaching 2.55 billion metric tons by the end of 2025. It also highlights persistent oversupply, trade restrictions, and new capacity additions in Asia and the Middle East as major forces shaping the market.

title: 3 innovations in green steel
summary: This article highlights recent developments in lower-carbon steelmaking, including hydrogen-based steel plants, Boston Metal’s molten oxide electrolysis approach, and Kobe Steel’s reduced-coke furnace technique. It focuses on how these technologies could help produce steel with much lower or near-zero CO2 emissions.

title: Smelting Barriers: The Innovators Forging a New Future for Iron and Steel
summary: The piece surveys emerging innovations aimed at cutting emissions across the iron and steel value chain, from process electrification to waste-stream valorization. It frames these technologies as part of a broader transition toward resilient, low-carbon industrial production.

title: Breakthrough technologies
summary: This page explains that there is no single route to low- or near-zero-emissions steelmaking and that multiple technologies will be needed. It discusses categories such as CCS, hydrogen-based routes, and electrolysis as pathways to more sustainable steel production.

title: Copper futures slip below $6.3/lb after recent gains
summary: Copper futures fell below $6.3 per pound on Friday, giving back some of their recent gains. The report attributes the move in part to Rio Tinto resuming exports of copper concentrate from its Oyu Tolgoi project, which improved supply expectations.

title: Copper prices continue to surge amid geopolitical tensions, supply shortages, and AI-driven demand
summary: Copper prices are rising on a combination of geopolitical tensions, structural supply shortages, and strong demand tied to the AI boom. The article says traders are increasingly treating copper as a strategic commodity with a prolonged supply crunch expected from underinvestment and long project lead times.

title: Copper hit a fresh record high, but metals trade may be stretched
summary: This segment says copper reached a fresh record high and crossed $13,000 a ton for the first time. It also raises the question of whether the metals rally has become overstretched after the rapid move higher.

title: Copper's role in the energy transition grows as demand surges
summary: This article says copper companies are responding to rising demand by increasing production, expanding exploration, and adopting sustainability practices. It also highlights digital innovation and circular economy approaches as key strategies for long-term value creation.

title: Rewiring a sustainable, clean energy future with copper
summary: The article explains how copper is central to the clean energy transition, with the International Energy Agency projecting demand for clean energy uses could nearly double by 2050. It frames copper as essential to electrification while also drawing attention to the sustainability challenges of expanding supply.

title: Copper in the Age of AI: Challenges of Electrification
summary: This report projects copper demand rising from 28 million metric tons in 2025 to 42 million metric tons by 2040, driven by electrification, AI data centers, solar, wind, and electric vehicles. It also notes that recycling could play a larger role, with higher recovery rates potentially adding about 6 million metric tons of recycled copper by 2040.

title: The role of copper in the energy transition
summary: DNV argues that copper scarcity could slow renewable energy deployment and recommends a response built around resource diversification, recycling, technology innovation, and collaboration. The piece emphasizes that more efficient copper use and better recycling are important to a sustainable energy transition.

title: Copper for Tomorrow: Sustainable copper production
summary: The article describes an effort aimed at solving value-chain challenges that limit sustainable copper production. It focuses on improving the way copper is produced so supply can grow while reducing environmental impacts.

title: Copper's Contribution to the Circular Economy
summary: This piece explains copper’s growing role in electromobility, energy efficiency, and renewable energy, and links that growth to circular economy benefits. It highlights recycling and reuse as important tools for supporting future copper demand more sustainably.

title: Nickel price and chart - Buy Nickel today on eToro
summary: This market page shows nickel trading data rather than a news article. It reports the current nickel price and recent percentage changes over the last 24 hours, week, and year.

title: Nickel Prices Ready to Rise as Supply Tightens in Fourth Quarter
summary: This video discusses nickel market conditions and argues that supply tightening could support higher prices later in the year. It highlights Indonesia-related supply shifts and notes that nickel has been range-bound since April 2025.

title: Nickel Futures Price Today - Investing.com
summary: This live commodities page reports nickel futures prices and recent movements. It shows nickel trading around the high-$17,000s per tonne, indicating a modest recovery from recent levels.

title: Nickel Prices 2026 - Chart, Trends Analysis & Forecast
summary: This pricing report provides regional nickel price updates and trend analysis. It says Northeast Asia and Europe saw price increases in May 2026, reflecting mixed but generally firmer market conditions.

title: Nickel's New Era: Rising Prices, Growing EV Demand ...
summary: This video explains how Indonesia’s supply management actions and rising EV demand are reshaping the nickel market. It argues that these factors have pushed prices higher and could support additional gains.

title: Nickel Spot Price Chart | Live Price of Nickel per Ounce
summary: This live price page provides nickel spot pricing and chart data. It is useful for tracking current market levels, but it does not appear to be a news article.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: This market page notes nickel trading around $17,900 per tonne after recovering from a two-month low. It attributes the move partly to concerns over Indonesia’s mining policies.

title: Price of Nickel per Pound
summary: This live metals page lists current nickel bid, ask, and tonnage prices. It is a pricing snapshot rather than a news report.

title: Nickel prices, news and market analysis
summary: This page focuses on nickel price trends, charts, news, and market analysis. It is a relevant market resource for tracking recent developments in the nickel sector.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: Bloomberg reports that PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world's biggest nickel mine in Indonesia, has been told to sharply reduce output. The report says the company was notified of a 12 million ton ore production quota for 2026, down from 42 million tons in 2025, as Indonesia seeks to support nickel prices.

title: Indonesia navigates nickel market with output cuts, policy shifts
summary: S&P Global reports that Indonesia confirmed plans to cut nickel production in 2026 to support prices and government revenue. The article also notes a broader policy shift toward tighter supply management and environmental enforcement.
